title: mentha arvensis f. neesiana
intro: Mentha arvensis f. neesiana, also known as Japanese peppermint, is a subspecies of the wild mint plant (Mentha arvensis) native to Japan and parts of Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Japanese peppermint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to boost energy and improve mental clarity. Japanese peppermint can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
